prerequisites: A Google account with access to the Google Calendar(s) you want to sync.
A Google Cloud project with the Google Calendar API enabled.
An OAuth 2.0 client ID and client secret created on the Google Cloud Console Credentials page.
An OAuth 2.0 refresh token obtained by authorizing the client with a Calendar scope and access_type=offline.
The calendar ID (calendarid) of the calendar to read; use 'primary' for the account's primary calendar or the calendar's address for a secondary calendar.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (refresh token)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with OAuth 2.0 using the client_id and client_secret of a Google Cloud OAuth client plus a long-lived refresh token (client_refresh_token_2). The connector exchanges the refresh token for access tokens to call the Calendar API on your behalf. Google issues a refresh token only when the authorization request uses access_type=offline.

setupSteps.title: Enable the Google Calendar API
setupSteps.body: In the Google Cloud Console, create or select a project and enable the Google Calendar API for it. This exposes the v3 Calendar endpoints the connector reads from.

setupSteps.title: Configure the OAuth consent screen
setupSteps.body: Configure the OAuth consent screen for the project: set the app information, choose the user type (internal or external), add contact details, and request a Calendar scope. Use https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.readonly for read-only access or https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar for full access. Prefer the most narrowly focused scope.

setupSteps.title: Create an OAuth 2.0 client ID and secret
setupSteps.body: On the Credentials page, create an OAuth 2.0 client ID. Record the generated client ID and client secret; provide these as the connector's client_id and client_secret fields. Keep the client secret confidential.

setupSteps.title: Obtain a refresh token
setupSteps.body: Authorize the OAuth client against the chosen Calendar scope with access_type=offline so Google returns a refresh token in addition to the access token. Supply this value as client_refresh_token_2. The refresh token stays valid until the user revokes access or it expires.

setupSteps.title: Set the calendar ID
setupSteps.body: Provide the calendarid for the calendar to sync. Use 'primary' for the account's primary calendar, or the calendar's address/ID for a secondary or shared calendar.

sources.title: Google Calendar API authentication and authorization
Using OAuth 2.0 for Web Server Applications
